meta.notes: Compliance data current as of mid-2026. Verify with linked official sources before relying on any figure for a compliance decision.

AL: No state law — federal minimum applies
CA: $20 for fast food workers; some locality rates higher
MI: Increasing to $12.48 by 2028 per court-ordered schedule
NE: Increases to $15 by Jan 2026; verify current rate
NV: Flat $12 for all employers since Jul 2024 (health benefit differential eliminated)
NJ: Indexed to CPI for large employers. Effective Jul 1, 2026
VA: Increasing to $15 by Jan 2026; verify current rate

AK.ag: AK Dept of Labor & Workforce Dev

HI.ag: HI Dept of Labor & Industrial Relations

MI.ag: MI Dept of Labor & Economic Opportunity

NV.ag: NV Office of the Labor Commissioner

ND.ag: ND Dept of Labor & Human Rights

OH.ag: OH Bureau of Workers Comp / Commerce

SC.ag: SC Dept of Labor, Licensing & Regulation

TN.ag: TN Dept of Labor & Workforce Dev

newsFeed.title: BREAKING: Fifth Circuit Vacates DOL $58,656 Overtime Threshold
newsFeed.desc: The Fifth Circuit vacated the DOL's 2024 rule raising the overtime exempt salary threshold to $58,656 per year, reverting to $35,568. Employers who reclassified workers as hourly should assess re-exemption options immediately. A DOL appeal or cert petition is expected.

newsFeed.title: EEOC AI Hiring Guidance Effective Aug 1 — 6 Weeks to Comply
newsFeed.desc: With the EEOC's final AI hiring guidance effective August 1, 2026, employers have six weeks to complete bias audits of algorithmic screening tools, update records retention policies, and train recruiters on accommodation pathways for candidates flagged by automated systems.

newsFeed.title: IL and NJ Minimum Wage — 9 Days to Jul 1 Effective Date
newsFeed.desc: Illinois at $15.60/hr and New Jersey at $15.90/hr take effect July 1. Chicago's local rate moves to $16.50/hr. Payroll systems must be updated by June 27 to ensure correct first-pay-period rates. Penalties apply immediately on July 1.

newsFeed.title: Delaware PFML Benefits Live — 9,400 Claims Processed Through May
newsFeed.desc: Delaware's Paid Leave program, active since January 2026, has processed approximately 9,400 claims through May 2026. The 0.8% contribution rate holds through 2027. Confirm employees have received required written notice and that deductions appear correctly on pay stubs.

newsFeed.title: Jun 2026 Preliminary ADP Data — 138K Private Sector Jobs Added
newsFeed.desc: Preliminary ADP data points to 138K private sector jobs added in June 2026, below consensus expectations of 160K. Healthcare and leisure and hospitality led gains; manufacturing shed 12K. The official BLS report publishes July 3, 2026.

newsFeed.title: EEO-1 Filing Portal Opens — Deadline July 15, 2026
newsFeed.desc: The EEOC opened the 2025 EEO-1 Component 1 data collection portal this week. Employers with 100 or more employees and federal contractors with 50 or more employees must file workforce demographic data by July 15, 2026. First-time filers should register at the EEOC portal now.

newsFeed.title: CO FAMLI Q2 Contribution Filing Due Jul 31
newsFeed.desc: Colorado FAMLI employers must submit Q2 2026 payroll and contribution reports by July 31, 2026. The combined rate remains 0.9% of wages. Employers who missed Q1 filings face penalties of 1.5% of overdue contributions per month plus interest.

newsFeed.title: Post-RTO Workforce Audits — Remote Nexus Compliance Gaps Found
newsFeed.desc: Following large-scale return-to-office mandates, HR compliance teams are discovering that employees who relocated during 2020 through 2024 created unresolved workers comp, unemployment insurance, and income tax nexus obligations in 30 or more states. DOL and state agencies are cross-referencing I-9 addresses with payroll filings.

newsFeed.title: DC Circuit Upholds NLRB Broader Joint Employer Standard
newsFeed.desc: The DC Circuit upheld the NLRB's 2023 joint employer rule expanding liability to businesses sharing or codetermining essential employment terms with staffing agencies. Employers relying on PEO or staffing firm arrangements should review contracts for indemnification gaps.

newsFeed.title: CA AB 3XX — Pay Scale Posting for Remote Roles Passes Assembly
newsFeed.desc: California AB 3XX, requiring salary range posting for roles that can be performed remotely anywhere in the US, passed the state Assembly and moves to Senate. If signed, it takes effect January 1, 2027 with significant implications for national job postings.

newsFeed.title: Maine PFML Benefits Now Available — Launch Confirmed Jun 1
newsFeed.desc: Maine's Paid Family and Medical Leave program began accepting benefit claims June 1, 2026. The 1% combined contribution rate applies. Employers with fewer than 15 employees are not required to pay the employer share. Employees may take up to 12 weeks of benefits.

newsFeed.title: JOLTS May 2026: 7.6M Openings — Quits Rate Hits 5-Year Low
newsFeed.desc: The May 2026 JOLTS report showed 7.6M job openings with the quits rate falling to 1.8%, the lowest since 2020. The hires rate dropped to 2.9%. A declining quits rate signals reduced worker confidence in finding better employment, a potential leading indicator of softening labor demand.

newsFeed.title: FTC Non-Compete — En Banc Petition Filed, 12 to 18 Month Timeline
newsFeed.desc: The FTC filed its en banc petition with the Eighth Circuit challenging the injunction blocking the federal non-compete ban. Legal observers estimate a 12 to 18 month timeline to resolution. State-level bans in CA, MN, ND, and OK apply now regardless of federal litigation.

newsFeed.title: NY Freelance Law — $2,500 Minimum Triggers Written Contract Requirement
newsFeed.desc: New York's expanded Freelance Isn't Free Act now covers engagements worth $2,500 or more over a 120-day period. Written contracts are mandatory; payments must be made within 30 days of completion. Civil penalties of up to $25,000 apply for violations.

newsFeed.title: 58% of HR Teams Lack a Formal AI Governance Policy — New Survey
newsFeed.desc: A June 2026 HR Policy Association survey found 58% of HR leaders lack a written AI governance policy covering hiring, performance management, or workforce planning tools. With the EEOC's August 1 effective date for AI hiring guidance, this gap is now a compliance risk.

AL.note: Above-average charge rate; retaliation and Title VII most common.

AK.note: Below-average charge rate; disability and retaliation most common.

AZ.note: Growing charge volume; retaliation and national origin prominent.

AR.note: Moderate charge rate; race and sex discrimination common.

CA.note: Highest absolute volume; DFEH/CRD parallel state enforcement adds complexity.

CO.note: Below-average rate; CDLE enforces parallel state law.

CT.note: Moderate volume; CHRO state enforcement active.

DE.note: High per-capita rate; small workforce amplifies numbers.

DC.note: Highest per-capita rate in US; DC HRA broadest protected classes; OHR active.

FL.note: Second highest absolute volume; high retaliation and disability charges.

GA.note: Very high per-capita rate; Atlanta metro drives volume; retaliation #1.

HI.note: Moderate rate; HCRC state enforcement alongside EEOC.

IL.note: High volume; IDHR state enforcement active; Chicago metro concentration.

KY.note: Above-average per-capita rate; KCHR state enforcement.

LA.note: High per-capita rate; race discrimination most common basis.

MD.note: High rate; MCHR state enforcement; federal contractor concentration.

MA.note: Moderate rate; MCAD state enforcement among most active in US.

MN.note: Moderate rate; MDHR state enforcement active.

MS.note: High per-capita rate; race discrimination most prominent.

NV.note: Very high per-capita rate; gaming/hospitality sector concentration.

NJ.note: High rate; DCR state enforcement very active.

NM.note: Moderate rate; national origin prominent given demographics.

NY.note: High absolute volume; DHR state enforcement among most active; NYC CCHR adds layer.

NC.note: High per-capita rate; retaliation and race most common.

ND.note: Lowest charge rate in US; small workforce.

OH.note: High volume; OCRC state enforcement active.

OR.note: Below-average rate; BOLI state enforcement active.

PA.note: High absolute volume; PHRC state enforcement.

SC.note: High per-capita rate; retaliation and race most common.

TN.note: High per-capita rate; retaliation and disability common.

TX.note: Highest absolute volume in US; large workforce; retaliation #1.

UT.note: Below-average rate; religion discrimination above national average.

VT.note: Low absolute volume; VHRC state enforcement.

VA.note: High rate; DOLI state enforcement; federal contractor concentration in NoVA.

WA.note: Moderate rate; WSHRC state enforcement active.

WY.note: Very low charge rate; small workforce.
